regions.us-ports: Today at Miami, easterly winds at 8 knots will develop under overcast skies with less than 10% rain probability, supporting normal embarkation and shore excursion operations. Heat Advisory in effect for Port Everglades (heat index up to 108°F), Baltimore (heat index up to 105°F), Manhattan and Brooklyn (heat index up to 104°F), Bayonne (heat index up to 101°F), Los Angeles and Long Beach (temperature up to 103°F), and San Diego (temperature up to 90°F); Extreme Heat Warning in effect for Philadelphia (heat index up to 107°F). Over the next 48 hours, southwesterly winds increase to 11 knots with drizzle and less than 10% rain probability on Day 2, then shift southerly at 15 knots on Day 3 as thunderstorms develop with 17% rain probability, which may affect afternoon port activities and outdoor excursions.
regions.bahamas-central-caribbean: Today elevated heat with index values reaching 100 degrees poses a serious risk to passengers during outdoor shore excursions across Nassau, Freeport, Bimini, Berry Islands, Key West, Grand Cayman, Ocho Rios, Falmouth, Puerto Plata, La Romana, Santo Domingo, and Samaná, with ENE winds at 9 knots and overcast skies limiting natural cooling. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, heat index values again reaching 100 degrees will persist across the region as ESE winds hold at 8 knots with continued overcast conditions and less than 10% rain probability, keeping all ports operationally clear for embarkation and disembarkation.
regions.eastern-caribbean: Today, easterly winds at 15 knots with clear skies and less than 10% rain probability will support normal port operations and outdoor shore excursions across San Juan, St. Thomas, St. Croix, St. Maarten, St. Kitts, and Antigua. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, easterly winds remain steady at 15 knots with drizzle developing; a 20% rain probability on Day 2 may affect timing of water-based excursions but will not disrupt embarkation or disembarkation at any port. Beyond 48 hours, easterly winds increase slightly to 16 knots on Day 3 with drizzle persisting and less than 10% rain probability, indicating stable conditions for cruise operations through the forecast period.
regions.western-caribbean: Today east winds at 11 knots with thunderstorms and 37% rain probability may affect shore excursions across Cozumel, Costa Maya, Roatan, and Belize City, though operations remain feasible with brief delays possible. Tomorrow brings east-northeast winds at 10 knots with continued thunderstorms and 27% rain probability, moderating conditions as the system weakens. By day three, east-northeast winds at 10 knots persist with drizzle and 11% rain probability, indicating improving stability across the region.
regions.southern-caribbean: Today easterly winds reach 21 knots with partly cloudy skies and 13% rain probability, supporting normal port operations and shore excursions across Aruba, Curacao, Bonaire, and Cartagena despite elevated winds. Day 2 through Day 3 maintain easterly winds at 21 knots with drizzle and less than 10% rain probability, allowing continued passenger activity with minimal operational disruption. Conditions remain stable beyond 48 hours with persistent east-northeast flow at 21 knots; no significant deterioration is forecast for the cruise region.
regions.lesser-antilles: Today easterly winds at 13 knots with drizzle and 12% rain probability will allow port operations and shore excursions across Barbados, St. Lucia, Martinique, Dominica, Antigua, St. Kitts, St. Maarten, St. Vincent, and Grenada to proceed normally without significant weather disruption. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, easterly winds remain steady at 13 knots with drizzle conditions and 10% rain probability, maintaining favorable conditions for embarkation and disembarkation at all Lesser Antilles ports. Beyond 48 hours, northeasterly winds increase slightly to 14 knots with drizzle and a 19% rain probability, still supporting routine cruise operations throughout the region.
regions.eastern-pacific: Today westerly winds at 10 knots with drizzle and 25% rain probability will produce minimal disruption to embarkation and shore excursions across Cabo San Lucas, Ensenada, Huatulco, Manzanillo, Mazatlan, and Puerto Vallarta. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, westerly winds increase to 12 knots with continued drizzle and 16% rain probability, though heat index values reaching 101 degrees on Day 2 will elevate passenger heat stress during outdoor activities, particularly at Cabo San Lucas and Puerto Vallarta where exposure is greatest.
regions.los-angeles: Today light southwest winds at 11 knots will develop under overcast skies with less than 10% rain probability, allowing normal port operations and shore excursions at Los Angeles, Long Beach, Marina del Rey, and Catalina Island. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, southeast winds will shift to 9 knots with fog moving into the region, though less than 10% rain probability maintains favorable conditions for embarkation and disembarkation activities. Beyond 48 hours, southeast winds will strengthen to 12 knots with overcast conditions returning and less than 10% rain probability, presenting stable cruising weather through the forecast period.
regions.ensenada: Today light southerly winds at 2 knots with partly cloudy skies and less than 10% rain probability will support normal port operations and shore excursion activities across Ensenada, Punta Banda, and Islas Todos Santos. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, westerly winds will increase to 8 knots while fog develops and thickens, potentially reducing visibility during embarkation and disembarkation at all three ports, though less than 10% rain probability persists. Beyond 48 hours, westerly winds will strengthen to 10 knots under overcast skies with less than 10% rain probability, maintaining generally stable conditions for cruise operations through the extended forecast period.
regions.cabo-san-lucas: Today WNW winds at 10 knots with partly cloudy skies and 25% rain probability will allow most port operations and shore excursions in Cabo San Lucas, San Jose del Cabo, and La Paz to proceed normally, though elevated heat index values reaching 101°F on Day 2 and 103°F on Day 3 will require passengers to take precautions during outdoor activities. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, WNW winds intensifying to 12 knots with drizzle and 16% rain probability will maintain generally favorable conditions for embarkation and disembarkation at all three ports.
regions.mazatlan: Today elevated heat with a heat index reaching 103F poses a serious risk to passengers during outdoor shore excursions across Mazatlan, Topolobampo, and Altata, with WNW winds at 7 knots and a 30% rain probability limiting thunderstorm impacts on port operations. Day 2 brings continued heat stress with a heat index of 102F and NW winds at 7 knots as thunderstorm activity increases to a 36% rain probability, which may affect tender operations and excursion timing. By Day 3, heat index values remain elevated at 102F with ESE winds at 8 knots and a 43% rain probability expected to impact outdoor activities and passenger comfort across the region.
regions.puerto-vallarta: Today excessive heat with a heat index reaching 106 degrees poses a serious risk to passengers during outdoor shore excursions across Puerto Vallarta, Punta Mita, Yelapa, and Chacala, with ENE winds at 9 knots and 24% rain probability limiting afternoon relief. Day 2 brings elevated heat with a heat index of 104 degrees and ESE winds at 8 knots as a 33% rain chance may affect port operations, while Day 3 shows continued elevated heat with a heat index of 100 degrees and northerly winds at 7 knots as a 46% rain probability could impact tender operations and excursion scheduling.
regions.western-mediterranean: Today south-southwest winds at 10 knots with overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability will allow smooth embarkation and disembarkation operations across Barcelona, Valencia, Palma de Mallorca, Ibiza, Malaga, Cadiz, Lisbon, and Gibraltar. Through tomorrow, southeast winds at 8 knots maintain overcast conditions with less than 10% rain probability, supporting continued port operations and shore excursions without weather delays. By day three, southeast winds increase to 10 knots under partly cloudy skies with less than 10% rain probability, with a heat index reaching 100 degrees in southern ports including Malaga and Cadiz, requiring passengers to take precautions during outdoor activities.
regions.central-mediterranean: Today across the Central Mediterranean, light southwest winds at 7 knots with partly cloudy skies and less than 10% rain probability support normal port operations and shore excursions at Marseille, Nice, Monaco, Genoa, La Spezia, Livorno, Civitavecchia, Naples, and the Sardinia and Corsica ports. Over the next 24-48 hours, southwest winds remain steady at 7 knots with partly cloudy conditions and less than 10% rain probability, maintaining favorable conditions for Split and Dubrovnik in the Adriatic.
regions.eastern-mediterranean: Today elevated heat with heat index values reaching 100 degrees will challenge passengers during outdoor shore excursions throughout the Eastern Mediterranean, particularly in Athens, Santorini, Rhodes, and Cyprus, while NNE winds at 6 knots and partly cloudy skies with less than 10% rain probability support normal port operations and embarkation activities. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, heat index conditions will persist at 100 degrees across all ports as NE winds strengthen to 9 knots with continued partly cloudy skies and less than 10% rain probability, maintaining favorable conditions for tendering and water-based excursions.
regions.southeast-alaska: Today, overcast skies with WNW winds at 2 knots and 19% rain probability will allow port operations and shore excursions in Juneau, Ketchikan, and Skagway to proceed without significant weather disruption. Over the next 48 hours, NNW winds at 5 knots on Day 1 and WNW winds at 5 knots on Day 2 will maintain light conditions across all ports, with drizzle and 19% rain probability on Day 1 followed by drizzle with less than 10% rain probability on Day 2. Beyond 48 hours, WNW winds at 6 knots and drizzle with less than 10% rain probability on Day 3 indicate continued stable weather with minimal operational impact.
